findings.description: HTML form code example is omitted. The source contains a complete HTML form demonstrating the multi-form naming convention (form.user.name, form.user.remarks, form.address.zipCode, etc.), which illustrates the correct usage pattern for implementing HTML forms with multi-form validation.
findings.source_evidence: Source section 複数の Form に対するバリデーション contains: `<form method="POST"><!-- ユーザの情報 -->ユーザ名: <input type="text" name="form.user.name"/>...郵便番号: <input type="text" name="form.address.zipCode"/>...`

findings.description: UserForm constructor `UserForm(Map<String, Object> params)` is omitted from the Form implementation code example. This constructor shows how to populate entity fields from request parameters, which is part of the correct implementation pattern.
findings.source_evidence: Source: `public UserForm(Map<String, Object> params) { user = (UserEntity) params.get("user"); address = (AddressEntity) params.get("address"); }`

findings.description: `ValidationContext` is missing from s2 hints despite appearing in the Form implementation code (`ValidationContext<Entity1>`) and Action code (`ValidationContext<Example2Form>`).
findings.source_evidence: Source s2 code: `@ValidateFor("validateForSample") public static void validateForSample(ValidationContext<Entity1> context)` and `ValidationContext<Example2Form> validationResult = ...`

findings.description: `validateAndConvertRequest` is missing from s2 hints despite being the primary API method used in the Action implementation example for this section.
findings.source_evidence: Source s2 Action section: `ValidationContext<Example2Form> validationResult = ValidationUtil.validateAndConvertRequest("form", Example2Form.class, request, "validateForSample");`
